title: astragalus exscapus subsp. exscapus
intro: Astragalus exscapus subsp. exscapus, also known as the alpine milk-vetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family Fabaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Astragalus exscapus subsp. exscapus is valued for its adaptogenic and immune-modulating properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties that can help mitigate the effects of stress and fatigue. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Astragalus exscapus subsp. exscapus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, capsules, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible supplement for those seeking its medicinal benefits.
